items.title: Show HN: I built a waitlist tool with Claude as a non-technical founder
items.why_it_matters: A non-technical founder used Claude to build and ship a complete waitlist tool, proving you don't need to over-analyze architecture to get a product into the wild. This week, ask Claude to generate a simple, high-converting waitlist page for AI-ingest using your Next.js + Supabase stack and deploy it to Vercel in under 30 minutes.

items.title: Show HN: I built a way to find and install Claude skills
items.why_it_matters: An indie developer built a directory to find and install custom Claude skills, solving a real discovery problem for builders who want to extend their AI workflows. This week, visit claudinho.xyz, find a skill tailored for Next.js or Supabase, and import it into your Claude workflow to speed up your coding sessions.

items.title: A harness for every task: dynamic workflows in Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic introduced dynamic workflows to Claude Code, allowing the agent to adapt its execution path based on real-time task feedback. This makes the tool you use every day much more reliable at handling complex, multi-step coding tasks without getting stuck. This week, update your Claude Code CLI to the latest version and ask it to write and run a test suite for your AI-ingest API routes.

items.title: Codex SDK – Programmatically control local Codex agents
items.why_it_matters: OpenAI released an SDK to programmatically control local Codex agents, making it easier to build custom developer workflows. Since you already use Codex, this lets you script your own automated coding assistants. This week, install the Codex SDK and write a 10-line script to automatically generate git commit messages based on your local workspace changes.

items.title: Show HN: Circus Chief – Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini from Your Phone
items.why_it_matters: This open-source tool lets you manage and schedule Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini agent sessions from a mobile-friendly web UI. It is perfect for keeping your builds running or monitoring agent progress when you are away from your laptop. This week, clone the Circus Chief repo, run it locally, and try triggering a minor refactoring task on your AI-ingest codebase from your phone.

items.title: Show HN: Paseo – Beautiful open-source coding agent interface
items.why_it_matters: Paseo is an open-source browser interface designed specifically for local coding agents like Claude Code. It gives you a clean visual workspace to review, accept, or reject code changes instead of relying entirely on terminal outputs. This week, install Paseo and connect it to your AI-ingest repo to see if a visual agent interface helps you direct Claude's edits more efficiently.

items.title: Codexia: "IDE" for Codex and Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: This is a lightweight, open-source IDE wrapper built specifically for orchestrating Codex and Claude Code sessions. It bridges the gap between terminal-only tools and massive IDEs, keeping your workspace focused. This week, download Codexia and use it to run your next Claude Code session to see if a dedicated agent workspace speeds up your development.

items.title: Show HN: Claude Code plugin for deep multi-agent code reviews
items.why_it_matters: This open-source plugin for Claude Code runs deep, multi-agent code reviews on your local repository. It acts as an automated pull-request reviewer to catch bugs before you push. This week, install the plugin and run a deep review on your Supabase integration code to verify your database queries are secure and optimized.

items.title: MDMA – Turn LLM Responses into Interactive UI via MCP
items.why_it_matters: MDMA is an MCP server that turns raw LLM responses into interactive, rendered UI components in real-time. This makes it incredibly easy to prototype and tweak frontend layouts interactively while chatting with Claude. This week, add the MDMA MCP server to your Claude configuration and use it to visually tweak the layout of your AI-ingest news feed.

items.title: We Reined In AI Agents With pre-commit
items.why_it_matters: An engineering team shared how they use standard Git pre-commit hooks to stop AI coding agents from committing broken or unformatted code. Since you are building Git discipline on AI-ingest, this is a highly practical way to keep your main branch clean. This week, set up a simple pre-commit hook in your AI-ingest repo that runs `npm run build` to ensure Claude Code never commits code that breaks your Vercel deployment.
